project.name: agentbreeder
project.description: Define Once. Deploy Anywhere. Govern Automatically. Open-source platform for building, deploying, and governing enterprise AI agents across multiple frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, Claude SDK, OpenAI Agents, Google ADK) and clouds (AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes) using a single agent.yaml. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.summary: Overlays environment variable values (AGENT_AUTH_TOKEN, OTLP_*, port, etc.) on top of the YAML-loaded Config struct.

nodes.name: config_test.go
nodes.summary: Comprehensive config tests covering defaults, env overrides, missing-file behavior, validation rules for auth/guardrails/MCP, and integer parsing.

nodes.name: cost.go
nodes.summary: Emits cost and audit events to the AgentBreeder API, posting token counts and metadata so deployments can be attributed to teams and agents.

nodes.name: Client
nodes.summary: HTTP client wrapper that POSTs to /api/v1/costs and /api/v1/audit using the configured base URL and bearer token.

nodes.name: Send

nodes.name: cost_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying Event validation, dual-endpoint posting, audit-failure resilience, and metadata propagation for the cost client.

nodes.name: guardrails.go
nodes.summary: Rule-based guardrail engine combining default PII regexes with user-provided block/redact rules; used by the proxy to inspect outbound payloads.

nodes.name: Evaluator
nodes.summary: Holds compiled guardrail rules and evaluates payload strings against them, returning a Decision describing block status and redactions.

nodes.name: New
nodes.summary: Builds an Evaluator by compiling user rules and optionally appending the built-in PII rule set.

nodes.name: Evaluate
nodes.summary: Walks every rule against the payload, short-circuits on block actions, and aggregates redactions and violations into a Decision.

nodes.name: compileRule
nodes.summary: Compiles a Rule definition into a runtime matcher, validating regex syntax, action keywords, and replacement metadata.

nodes.name: guardrails_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests covering default PII redaction, user block/regex/keyword rules, invalid regex, unknown actions, short-circuit blocks, and rule introspection.

nodes.name: mcp.go
nodes.summary: MCP passthrough client that forwards JSON-RPC payloads from the agent to configured upstream MCP servers over HTTP/SSE, returning structured JSON-RPC errors on failure.

nodes.name: Client
nodes.summary: MCP client struct holding the server registry and a shared HTTP transport.

nodes.name: NewClient
nodes.summary: Constructs an MCP Client indexed by server name with a configurable HTTP timeout.

nodes.name: Forward
nodes.summary: Dispatches a JSON-RPC payload to the named MCP server, rejecting stdio transport (sidecar only supports HTTP/SSE).

nodes.name: forwardHTTP
nodes.summary: Performs the actual HTTP POST to the upstream MCP server, threading auth headers and returning the upstream response body verbatim.

nodes.name: mcp_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for MCP forwarding including HTTP success, unknown-server lookup, stdio rejection, upstream 5xx errors, and JSON-RPC error envelope shape.

nodes.name: otelx.go
nodes.summary: OpenTelemetry OTLP/HTTP exporter that creates spans, encodes them per OTLP v1, and ships them to the configured collector with attribute typing for strings/ints/floats/bools.

nodes.name: Exporter
nodes.summary: OTLP/HTTP exporter that serializes Spans to OTLP protobuf-equivalent JSON and POSTs them to a collector endpoint.

nodes.name: New
nodes.summary: Constructs an OTLP Exporter from an endpoint, optional headers, and service ID; returns a no-op exporter when endpoint is empty.

nodes.name: Export
nodes.summary: Encodes a Span into the OTLP traces payload and POSTs it to the collector, surfacing 4xx/5xx responses to the caller.

nodes.name: encode
nodes.summary: Serializes a Span into the OTLP resourceSpans/scopeSpans/spans envelope including trace/span IDs, timestamps, attributes, and status.

nodes.name: otlpAttrs
nodes.summary: Maps Go attribute values to OTLP typed value variants (stringValue, intValue, doubleValue, boolValue).

nodes.name: otelx_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the OTLP exporter covering disabled no-ops, payload shape, header propagation, collector errors, span ID uniqueness, endpoint normalization, and attribute typing.

nodes.name: proxy.go
nodes.summary: Reverse proxy that forwards inbound requests to the agent container while inspecting bodies through the guardrails Evaluator and applying redactions or 403 blocks.

nodes.name: Proxy
nodes.summary: Reverse proxy struct wrapping httputil.ReverseProxy with an attached guardrails Evaluator.

nodes.name: New
nodes.summary: Builds a Proxy by parsing the target agent URL and pre-compiling the guardrail rules into an Evaluator.

nodes.name: ServeHTTP
nodes.summary: Inspects request bodies (when present), runs guardrails, blocks on rule violations, rewrites redacted strings back into JSON, and forwards to the agent.

nodes.name: extractStrings
nodes.summary: Walks a JSON body recursively and concatenates every string leaf so guardrails can scan the entire payload at once.

nodes.name: walkReplace
nodes.summary: Recursively rewrites every string leaf in a JSON value using a redaction mapping so guardrail-applied changes are reflected in the proxied body.

nodes.name: proxy_test.go
nodes.summary: Integration-style tests spinning up an httptest target to verify forwarding, guardrail blocking, body redaction, bad-gateway behavior, and target URL parsing.

nodes.name: server.go
nodes.summary: Top-level sidecar HTTP server wiring auth, proxy, A2A, MCP, cost, OTLP, and guardrails into a chi router with health/openapi endpoints and graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: Server
nodes.summary: Composite Server struct holding configured A2A, MCP, cost, OTLP, guardrails, and proxy components plus the chi router and shutdown context.

nodes.name: New
nodes.summary: Wires every sidecar subsystem (auth, proxy, A2A, MCP, cost, OTLP, guardrails) into a Server using the validated Config.

nodes.name: Run
nodes.summary: Starts the HTTP server on the configured port, blocks on context cancellation, and triggers graceful shutdown with a deadline.

nodes.name: handleA2A
nodes.summary: Local-router endpoint that decodes a JSON-RPC request and forwards it through the A2A client to the named peer.

nodes.name: handleMCP
nodes.summary: Local-router endpoint that proxies an MCP JSON-RPC payload to the configured MCP server by name.

nodes.name: handleCost
nodes.summary: Local-router endpoint that accepts a cost Event from the agent and forwards it to the AgentBreeder API for billing/audit.

nodes.name: buildA2APeers
nodes.summary: Translates Config A2A peer specs into a2a.Peer entries, falling back to a shared bearer token when a peer doesn't supply its own.

nodes.name: server_test.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end tests booting the sidecar against fake agent/A2A/MCP/cost servers to validate health, auth-gated proxy, A2A/MCP/cost handlers, OpenAPI, lifecycle, and listener errors.

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.summary: Falls back to building a compliance report from local audit-log data when the API is unavailable, structuring it per the requested standard.

nodes.name: list_standards
nodes.summary: Prints a table of all supported compliance standards (SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR, etc.) with their human-readable descriptions.

nodes.name: deploy.py

nodes.name: eject

layers.name: Documentation & Governance
layers.description: Top-level project documentation (README, ARCHITECTURE, ROADMAP, CLAUDE, CONTRIBUTING, GOVERNANCE), GitHub issue/PR templates, and root-level project configuration (pyproject.toml, alembic.ini, mkdocs.yml, .env.example) that define how AgentBreeder is built and contributed to.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Production Dockerfiles (api, cli, sidecar, dashboard), Docker Compose stacks (quickstart, e2e, override, standalone, production), Postgres init / LiteLLM / seed data, Alembic migration env, repo automation scripts, and GitHub Actions workflows that build, test, evaluate, release, and publish docs.

layers.name: CLI
layers.description: The Typer-based `agentbreeder` command — entrypoint plus 30+ subcommands (deploy, init, validate, scan, eject, schedule, eval, submit, review, publish, secret, template, etc.) that drive the deploy pipeline from the terminal.

layers.name: Engine — Deploy Pipeline Core
layers.description: DeployEngine orchestrator (`builder.py`), `config_parser.py`, `resolver.py`, `governance.py`, and `orchestrator.py` — the heart of the sacred parse → RBAC → resolve → build → deploy → register pipeline.

layers.name: Engine — Deployers
layers.description: Cloud deployer adapters for AWS ECS Fargate / App Runner, GCP Cloud Run, Azure Container Apps, Kubernetes, Docker Compose, and Claude Managed Agents, plus per-agent IAM identity and MCP sidecar injection.

layers.name: Engine — Runtimes & LLM Providers
layers.description: Framework-specific container builders (LangGraph, CrewAI, Claude SDK, OpenAI Agents, Google ADK, custom, polyglot Go/Node/Python) and the LLM provider abstraction (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Ollama, OpenAI-compatible, LiteLLM) with fallback chains.

layers.name: Engine — Platform Services
layers.description: Cross-cutting engine modules: JSON / OpenAPI schemas, A2A JSON-RPC protocol, sidecar injection glue, standard tools (markdown_writer, web_search, bridge/resolver/runner), pluggable secrets backends (env, AWS, GCP, Vault, keychain), MCP packaging, compliance scanning, and first-boot seed.

layers.name: Sidecar (Go Binary)
layers.description: The standalone Go cross-cutting-concerns sidecar — bearer-token auth, guardrails / PII filter, A2A JSON-RPC client, MCP HTTP/SSE passthrough, OTel exporter, cost & audit emission, reverse-proxy, and chi server assembly — auto-injected next to deployed agents.

layers.name: Registry
layers.description: Catalog service that performs CRUD over registry tables for agents, prompts, tools, models, providers, deploys, RAG indexes, MCP servers, A2A agents, and templates — the only sanctioned writer to registry tables.

layers.name: API — Routes & App Shell
layers.description: FastAPI entrypoint (`main.py`), settings, async SQLAlchemy setup, JWT/OAuth2 auth, RBAC middleware, API versioning, background tasks, plus REST endpoints under `/api/v1` and `/api/v2` (agents, deploys, prompts, providers, RAG, memory, git, sandbox, teams, costs, audit, tracing, builders, orchestrations, evals, playground, marketplace, A2A, agentops, gateway, MCP servers, templates).

layers.name: API — Services
layers.description: Business-logic service layer between routes and the registry — orchestration, evaluation, marketplace, A2A, sandbox, RAG, memory, git workflow, and template services.

layers.name: API — Models & Database Schema
layers.description: SQLAlchemy ORM models, Pydantic request/response schemas, and the 31-table registry schema (agents, deploys, prompts, costs, audit, teams, tracing, etc.) — the persistence contract for the entire platform.

layers.name: Connectors
layers.description: Pluggable third-party integrations: Datadog, Grafana, LiteLLM gateway, MCP scanner, OpenRouter, browser-use, SMTP email, and news (HackerNews, ArXiv, RSS).

layers.name: SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Go)
layers.description: Public client libraries: `agentbreeder-sdk` Python (`agenthub` package), `@agentbreeder/sdk` TypeScript, and a Go client — exposing agent, deploy, model, tool, memory, MCP, and orchestration primitives.

layers.name: Dashboard — Shell, Primitives & Lib
layers.description: React app entrypoint, root layout, build configuration (Vite, Tailwind, ESLint, Playwright, nginx, Dockerfile), shadcn/ui design-system primitives, custom hooks, and the `lib/` API client / agent.yaml emitters / graph-to-YAML transformers.

layers.name: Dashboard — Visual Builders
layers.description: ReactFlow-powered No Code visual builders for agents and multi-agent orchestrations, including node palettes, canvases, deploy wizard, and graph-to-YAML emitters that compile to `agent.yaml` / `orchestration.yaml`.

layers.name: Dashboard — Pages & Domain Components
layers.description: Top-level route components plus higher-level domain UI (registry pickers, RAG / memory editors, prompt test panels, agent cards, marketplace browser) — one component per dashboard page across Agents, Deploys, Prompts, Tools, Models, Providers, Costs, Audit, Tracing, Playground, Evals, Marketplace, and more.

tour.title: Project Orientation
tour.description: Start with the README to learn the elevator pitch — Define Once, Deploy Anywhere, Govern Automatically — and then read ARCHITECTURE.md and CLAUDE.md to internalize the sacred deploy pipeline that the rest of the tour will trace step-by-step. ROADMAP.md shows where the project is heading. Together these four docs frame every code decision you'll see in the next steps: framework-agnostic, multi-cloud, governance-as-side-effect.

tour.title: The agent.yaml Contract
tour.description: Everything in AgentBreeder revolves around one file: agent.yaml. This JSON Schema is the canonical definition of every field a user can write — identity, model, framework, tools, knowledge bases, prompts, guardrails, deploy target, access control. Treat it as the API contract between humans and the deploy pipeline; every other file in the engine either produces, validates, or consumes data shaped by this schema.
tour.languageLesson: JSON Schema's `oneOf`, `additionalProperties: false`, and `$ref` let you express discriminated unions and closed object shapes that pair well with Pydantic models on the Python side — the schema and the dataclasses validate the same input twice, from two angles.

tour.title: CLI Entry — Where the User Story Begins
tour.description: The user experience starts at the terminal. cli/main.py is a Typer application that registers every subcommand (deploy, init, validate, scan, eject, etc.), prints the welcome banner, and handles --version. cli/commands/up.py implements the local-stack bootstrap (`agentbreeder up`) — Docker prerequisite checks, .env generation, compose orchestration — which is most contributors' first interaction with the platform.
tour.languageLesson: Typer leans on Python type hints to generate CLI argument parsing, help text, and shell completion automatically — the function signature *is* the command spec, eliminating the boilerplate Click requires.

tour.title: The Deploy Command
tour.description: `agentbreeder deploy` is the product. cli/commands/deploy.py is a thin shim — it loads the YAML, identifies the user, and hands off to the DeployEngine. Notice how short this file is: the CLI does almost nothing except shape stdout/stderr. All the real work lives in engine/builder.py, which we'll meet next. This separation is deliberate so the API server, the dashboard, and the SDK can all invoke the same pipeline without going through the CLI.

tour.title: The Sacred Pipeline — DeployEngine
tour.description: engine/builder.py is the heart of AgentBreeder. It executes the immutable deploy pipeline: Parse YAML → RBAC Check → Resolve Dependencies → Build Container → Provision Infra → Deploy → Auto-Register → Return Endpoint URL. Every step is atomic; if any step fails, the whole thing rolls back. This file orchestrates the four engine helpers that follow — config_parser, governance, resolver — and finally hands off to a runtime + deployer pair.

tour.title: Parse, Govern, Resolve
tour.description: Three helpers run before any container gets built. config_parser.py loads agent.yaml, validates it against the JSON Schema from Step 2, and produces a Pydantic AgentConfig. governance.py is the RBAC gate — it raises RBACDeniedError if the user can't deploy under the agent's team, blocking the pipeline before a single byte of compute is wasted. resolver.py walks every registry reference (prompts, tools, KBs, A2A peers) and produces the fully hydrated bundle the builder will package.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 lets you encode validation rules (regex, ranges, cross-field constraints) as part of the model itself — `field_validator` and `model_validator` give you machine-readable error paths that the CLI surfaces as friendly messages.

tour.title: Framework Runtimes — Six Containers, One Interface
tour.description: AgentBreeder is framework-agnostic because of engine/runtimes/. base.py defines the RuntimeBuilder interface — validate, build, get_entrypoint, get_requirements — and each framework (LangGraph, CrewAI, Claude SDK, OpenAI Agents, Google ADK, custom) implements it independently. The DeployEngine never knows which framework it's packaging; it just calls `runtime.build()`. This is how you can swap LangGraph for CrewAI by changing one YAML line and nothing else breaks.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `abc.ABC` + `@abstractmethod` is the project's preferred way to model framework abstractions — every concrete runtime is forced to implement the same surface area, so the engine can call them polymorphically.

tour.title: Cloud Deployers — Four Clouds, One Interface
tour.description: Once the runtime has built a container, engine/deployers/ lands it on a cloud. base.py defines BaseDeployer (provision, deploy, health_check, teardown, get_logs). aws_ecs.py provisions ECR + Secrets Manager + an ECS Fargate task; gcp_cloudrun.py mirrors the same pattern with Artifact Registry + Cloud Run; kubernetes.py applies Deployment + Service + HPA manifests via the K8s Python client. Same interface as the runtimes — that's how Track K shipped multi-cloud first as an *equal* class.

tour.title: The Sidecar — Governance as a Side Effect
tour.description: The sidecar (Track J) is the magic that makes governance automatic. sidecar/cmd/sidecar/main.go is the entry point for a single Go binary that gets injected as a second container next to your agent — every deployer (ECS, Cloud Run, K8s) wires it in transparently. The sidecar emits OpenTelemetry traces, enforces guardrails (PII filter, content filter), proxies A2A and MCP traffic, validates bearer tokens, and writes cost + audit rows. Your YAML never mentions it; you simply get observability, security, and cost tracking for free.
tour.languageLesson: Go's small static binary + chi router pattern is ideal for sidecars: a few MB of distroless image, no runtime dependency, and you can ship the same binary to amd64 and arm64 from one Dockerfile.

tour.title: Registry & API — The Catalog Side
tour.description: After the deploy pipeline succeeds, the agent gets registered. api/main.py is the FastAPI shell that wires every v1/v2 router; api/routes/agents.py and api/routes/deploys.py are the HTTP surface for CRUD, invocation, and deploy-job orchestration. registry/agents.py is the service layer that actually persists agent records and version snapshots. By contract, only this layer (or the deployer) writes to the registry — never random application code. That guarantee keeps the catalog consistent.

tour.title: Dashboard — The No-Code Tier
tour.description: dashboard/src/App.tsx is the React + TypeScript shell — BrowserRouter wiring ~50 page components for agents, prompts, tools, deploys, traces, evals, marketplace, and governance. It's the No-Code surface from the three-tier model: visual builders generate valid agent.yaml, layout metadata lives in .agentbreeder/layout.json (never in agent.yaml), and the same deploy pipeline from Step 5 runs underneath. The dashboard is auth-guarded and talks to the FastAPI server from Step 10 via React Query.
tour.languageLesson: React Router's nested route + protected-route pattern keeps auth checks declarative — the App.tsx tree maps URLs to page components without scattering `if (!user) redirect()` calls into every page.

tour.title: SDKs & Connectors — Power Users
tour.description: Beyond YAML and the dashboard, agentbreeder ships a Full-Code tier: the Python SDK (sdk/python/agenthub/agent.py exposes Agent and AgentConfig) and the TypeScript SDK (sdk/typescript/src/index.ts re-exports Agent, Tool, deploy, validation). Both compile down to agent.yaml — they don't bypass the parser. connectors/base.py is the BaseConnector contract that every integration plugin (Datadog, Grafana, browser-use, SMTP, news feeds) implements, giving the engine a uniform way to talk to external systems.

tour.title: Local Stack & Distribution
tour.description: Finally, the operational glue. service:Dockerfile builds the production API image; deploy/docker-compose.quickstart.yml is the all-in-one stack (postgres, redis, chromadb, neo4j, MCP servers, migrate, api, dashboard, litellm) that `agentbreeder up` from Step 3 boots locally. .github/workflows/release.yml is the release pipeline — tag-triggered, it publishes both PyPI packages, pushes amd64+arm64 Docker images for api/dashboard/sidecar/cli, and updates the Homebrew tap. Three distribution channels, one workflow.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions matrix builds + PyPI Trusted Publishers (OIDC) eliminate long-lived release secrets — the workflow proves its identity to PyPI on each tag push without ever holding an API token in a GitHub secret.
